opinion.h2: J. J. BROUSSARD, Plaintiff in Error, v. R. R. BAKER,1 Chief of Police of the City of Beaumont, Tex. No. 199.
Supreme Court of the United States
opinion.p: Dismissed for want of jurisdiction upon the authority of (1) Consolidated Turnp. Co. v. Norfolk & O. V. R. Co. 228 U.S. 596, 600, 57 S. L. ed. 982, 983, 33 Sup. Ct. Rep. 609; Manhattan L. Ins. Co. v. Cohen, 234 U.S. 123, 137, 58 S. L. ed. 1245, 1254, 34 Sup. Ct. Rep. 874; Easterling Lumber Co. v. Pierce, 235 U.S. 380, 382, 59 S. L. ed. 279, 281, 35 Sup. Ct. Rep. 133; (2) Fischer v. St. Louis, 194 U.S. 361, 48 L. Ed. 1018, 24 Sup. Ct. Rep. 673; Davis v. Massa-

chusetts, 167 U.S. 43, 42 L. Ed. 71, 17 Sup. Ct. Rep. 731; (3) Iowa C. R. Co. v. Iowa, 160 U.S. 389, 40 L. Ed. 467, 16 Sup. Ct. Rep. 344; Washington v. Miller, 235 U.S. 422, 429, 59 S. L. ed. 295, 299, 35 Sup. Ct. Rep. 119; Roby v. South Park Comrs. 238 U.S. 610, 59 L. Ed. 1488, 35 Sup. Ct. Rep. 791.
Mr. Frederick S. Tyler for plaintiff in error.
No appearance for defendant in error.

f.p: Death of N. N. Smith, and appointment of his successor, R. R. Baker, as chief of police of the city of Beaumont, Texas, suggested, and appearance of R. R. Baker, as the party defendant in error herein, filed and entered on November 29, 1915.
